rationale: Forecast onset 8 days late. Deficit year likely. Short-season drought-tolerant variety essential. Avoid long-season millet.
Early onset with above-average rainfall. Waterlogging risk at low-lying plots. Use ridges. H614D tolerates wet better than DK8031.
Wetter than average forecast. Flash flood risk at river-proximate fields. Do not plant below 1800m elevation this season. Lodging risk — use Quncho.
Late onset + below-normal rainfall. Sorghum preferred over maize this season. Kapaala variety performs well under water stress.
Near-normal onset. No planting date shift. Slight waterlogging risk at valley bottom plots in June-July window.

Very late onset with deficit forecast. HIGH risk season. Consider fallowing 50% of land. Plant only drought-hardy short-season variety. Probability of crop failure above 40% if onset delayed past Nov 15.
Below-normal rainfall year. Late onset. Only 90-day millet viable — longer season risks hitting dry end-of-rains. Skip groundnut this season.
Near-normal season. Slightly early onset. No major shift. Above-average bean yield expected if fall armyworm managed.
